growth_records.cite: He et al. Design of a sorbitol-activated nitrogen metabolism-dependent regulatory system for redirection of carbon metabolism flow in Bacillus licheniformis. Nucleic Acids Research (2023). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkad859. [PMC10681722]
growth_records.snippet: under a consistent rate of glucose uptake, the specific productivity of acetoin decreased by 39.2%, from 0.34 to 0.21 g DCW/h ... both BL groups reached a maximum titer of 20.2 g/l ... 2.6 g/l produced by the wild-type strain (BL + sorbitol
growth_records.notes: Aerobic shake-flask, 60 g/L initial glucose, +20 g/L sorbitol. Wild-type BL specific acetoin productivity qP = 0.34 g/gDCW/h (reported, Fig 7D). Acetoin max titer 20.2 g/L; acetate 2.6 g/L; final broth pH 7.2. No µ or qS(glucose) numeric value reported in text (glucose uptake stated 'consistent' but not quantified). Titers listed under yields as absolute concentrations (not rates).

growth_records.cite: He et al. Design of a sorbitol-activated nitrogen metabolism-dependent regulatory system for redirection of carbon metabolism flow in Bacillus licheniformis. Nucleic Acids Research (2023). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkad859. [PMC10681722]
growth_records.snippet: a 57.6% decrease in acetoin production (from 8.73 to 3.7 g/l), and a 2.3-fold increase in acetate production (from 0.76 to 2.53 g/l) ... acetate exhibited the most significant alteration, increasing from 0.03 to 0.18 g/g glucose
growth_records.notes: Anaerobic wild-type BL. Acetoin titer 8.73 g/L, acetate 0.76 g/L, lactate undetectable. Acetate yield = 0.03 g/g glucose (reported). No specific rate (g/gDCW/h) or µ reported for anaerobic condition; titers listed but flagged as concentrations, not rates. product_yield acetate 0.03 g/g glucose is genuine flux-relevant datum.

growth_records.strain: WX-02/pHY300 (control, CCTCC M208065 harboring empty pHY300PLK)

mfa.note: 13C-MFA fluxes in Figure 4A and Table 3 normalized to glucose uptake = 100 for control strain
Table 3, control WX-02/pHY300; oxidative PPP NADPH

product_yields.carbon_recovery: 31.04 mmol C/g/h vs 43.38 mmol C/g/h consumed (71.6%)

growth_records.cite: Penghui He et al. 13C-Metabolic Flux Analysis Reveals the Metabolic Flux Redistribution for Enhanced Production of Poly-gamma-Glutamic Acid in dlt Over-Expressed Bacillus licheniformis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00105. [PMC6367249]
growth_records.snippet: WX-02/pHY300: Specific growth rate mu 0.29 h-1; Glucose uptake rate 7.23; gamma-PGA formation 0.65; Acetate 3.48; Acetoin 1.12; 2,3-Butanediol 1.14 mmol/g/h; carbon recovery 31.04.
growth_records.notes: Control strain (empty vector). mu 0.29 h-1 reported (Table 2). Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.29 = 2.390 h. Uptake/secretion rates measured, Table 2 (mmol/g/h = mmol/gDCW/h). Carbon recovery per paper: glucose C in = 7.23*6 = 43.38 mM/g/h; C in products+biomass = 0.65*5 + 3.48*2 + 1.12*4 + 1.14*4 + (0.29/24.6)*1000 = 31.04 (biomass formula C1H1.8O0.5N0.2, MW ~24.6). 13C-MFA net carbon flux distribution in Figure 4A (numeric per-reaction values in figure, not tabular); Table 3 cofactor-balance fluxes captured in mfa_fluxes, normalized to glucose=100.

growth_records.strain: WX-02/pHY-dltB (dltB overexpression; P43 promoter, amyL terminator)

mfa.note: 13C-MFA fluxes in Figure 4B and Table 3 normalized to glucose uptake = 100 for engineered strain
Table 3, engineered; PPP flux increased vs control (67.54)
Table 3, engineered; EMP slightly depressed vs control
Table 3, engineered; TCA strengthened vs control (79.68)
Table 3, engineered; vs -7.77 in control (higher gamma-PGA synthesis)

product_yields.carbon_recovery: 31.39 mmol C/g/h vs 43.92 mmol C/g/h consumed (71.5%)

growth_records.cite: Penghui He et al. 13C-Metabolic Flux Analysis Reveals the Metabolic Flux Redistribution for Enhanced Production of Poly-gamma-Glutamic Acid in dlt Over-Expressed Bacillus licheniformis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00105. [PMC6367249]
growth_records.snippet: WX-02/pHY-dltB: Specific growth rate mu 0.32 h-1; Glucose uptake 7.32; gamma-PGA formation 0.92; Acetate 3.57; Acetoin 0.56; 2,3-Butanediol 1.10 mmol/g/h; carbon recovery 31.39.
growth_records.notes: dltB-overexpression strain. mu 0.32 h-1 reported (Table 2). td = ln2/0.32 = 0.693/0.32 = 2.166 h. Uptake/secretion measured (Table 2, mmol/g/h). Carbon recovery per paper: glucose C in = 7.32*6 = 43.92 mM/g/h; C in products+biomass = 0.92*5 + 3.57*2 + 0.56*4 + 1.10*4 + (0.32/24.6)*1000 = 31.39. gamma-PGA rate up 0.65->0.92 (+41%) vs control; 13C-MFA shows enhanced PPP+TCA, depressed EMP. Table 3 cofactor fluxes captured; per-reaction carbon fluxes in Figure 4B (figure-only).
